zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Grey於1397年2月20日出生在威爾士丹比爾盧時，她的父親Reynold Grey II35歲，母親Elizabeth de Ros32歲。她和William Bonville至少有1個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1427年在英國薩默塞特修頓 門迪普享年30歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭薩默塞特修頓 門迪普。

nameMeanings.name: Grey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Gray  .
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Ukrainian Grushko: nickname or topographic name derived from a Russian-influenced transliteration of grushka ‘pear, pear tree’.

variants.name: Gray
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Greiwe
meanings: German: possibly a nickname from Middle High German greibe ‘sharp, tangy, dour’. Compare Grey .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Grey was born on 20 February 1397, in Ruthin, Denbighshire, Wales, her father, Reynold Grey II, was 35 and her mother, Elizabeth de Ros, was 32. She had at least 1 son and 3 daughters with William Bonville. She died in 1427, in Chewton Mendip, Somerset, England, at the age of 30, and was buried in Chewton Mendip, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Grey est née le 20 février 1397, à Ruthin, Denbighshire, Galles, son père, Reynold Grey II, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de Ros, avait 32 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles avec William Bonville. Elle est décédée en 1427, à Chewton Mendip, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 30 ans, et a été enterrée à Chewton Mendip, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Grey am 20. Februar 1397 in Ruthin, Denbighshire, Wales geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Reynold Grey II 35 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth de Ros 32. Sie hatte mit William Bonville mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1427 im Alter von 30 Jahren in Chewton Mendip, Somerset, England und wurde in Chewton Mendip, Somerset, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Greyが1397年2月20日にウェールズデンビグhシュアイアルスインで生まれた時，父Reynold Grey IIは35歳，母Elizabeth de Rosは32歳であった。彼女はWilliam Bonvilleとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1427年にイギリスソメルセトチェウトン メンディップで30歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスソメルセトチェウトン メンディップにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Grey(이)가 1397년 2월 20일 웨일스 덴비그h실르 루스인에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Reynold Grey II(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth de Ros(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 William Bonville(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1427년 영국 서머세트 체w턴 멘디프에서 30세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 체w턴 멘디프에 묻혔다.

У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери от William Bonville.
